notes: SHIP v1.14.255 — THE INSPECTION BAY: the .218 wireframe rack scene behind the FLAT|3D toggle is replaced by the John-locked Inspection Bay (MOCKUP-INSPECT3D-BAY-LOCKED.html + HANDOFF-INSPECT3D-BAY-PORT.md, both committed to /mocks/ this session). TWO PARTS. PART 1 — HOIST (3 edits): TYPE_COLOR / TLABEL / _TMAP moved from inside forge3d_render to module scope so the Bay and the Forge tray read ONE canonical colour map. Values BYTE-IDENTICAL to .254; only scope widened. This is the structural reason the Bay can satisfy the port's §E ('the bay must never contradict the elevation') — the next colour John rules moves both surfaces at once, no drift (the .251->.253 media saga is exactly the drift this prevents). PART 2 — THE PORT (1 spliced block replacing the .218 scene body, lines 31771-31863; interaction/orbit/raycast/scrub/teardown BELOW it kept verbatim per the spec). The block builds: scene+renderer+camera fresh (alpha:true, ACES, exposure 1.1, sRGB, FOV 28 = the mock's John-locked framing, no solid bg/fog — the env cubemap + backdrop carry depth); the §A LOCKED LIGHT RIG verbatim (ambient 0x1a2535@0.04, key 0xd4e5f7@0.78 pos(5.45,13.79,4.91) castShadow, fill 0x4a3a7a@0.10, rim 0x00d4aa@0.42, glow 0x00d4aa@0.08 r35, underGlow 0xa855f7@0.14 r20); the §B procedural dark-studio env CUBEMAP with envMapIntensity 0.25 everywhere; the rack STRUCTURE in chrome black (0x0c0f14 metal .92 rough .16); Master-driven TRAYS; and all nine §D bay elements (glass platform + calibration marks + gliding sweep bar, the SCAN WAVE 6.5s cycle with halo ring + traveling PointLight, ghost fleet 6/side + shelves + back rank, 220 data motes, glossy floor, ghost reflection clone, backdrop glow, invisible practicals, ~50s sweep light) and the §C HIGHLIGHT BAND (ghost fill 0x00d4aa + RESTORED edge box 0x1fffd0, breathing). ⭐ THE HIGHLIGHT EDGE BOX REVERSES A DOCUMENTED EARLIER RULING (bright edge box previously removed) — reversed by John, recorded here per §C's own instruction. ⚠️⚠️ ONE DELIBERATE DEVIATION FROM THE LOCKED MOCK, FLAGGED LOUD, JOHN'S CALL: the mock's chrome-black finish repaints EVERY tray piano-black (0x0c0f14), which would ERASE the type colours John ruled across .251-.253 (magenta UNKNOWN, slate MEDIA CONV, gold PDU). §E requires the bay to SHOW type colour and never contradict the elevation. These two cannot both hold. RESOLUTION: chrome black applied to the RACK STRUCTURE only; TRAYS KEEP THEIR TYPE COLOUR (it is information, not finish — the entire .238->.253 honesty arc is about that colour meaning something). Each tray is an emissive-tinted MeshStandard in TYPE_COLOR[_TMAP[rawType]||'unknown'], with a type-hued status LED. If John wants full piano-black trays like the mock preview, that is a ONE-FLAG change — but it would contradict the flat elevation and bury four ships of colour rulings, so it is NOT shipped without his word. ⭐ HGT-UNKNOWN HAZARD IN 3D (§E): a slot whose height is a 1U placeholder (hgtUnknown) gets a GOLD additive wireframe hatch = 'DO NOT TRUST THIS GEOMETRY', the 3D echo of the flat elevation's gold hatch. Distinct from an UNKNOWN *type* (magenta); both can co-occur on one tray, which is correct. GL DISCIPLINE (§E, VERIFIED not assumed): the Bay reuses the socket's untouched teardown, which disposes renderer + forceContextLoss, cross-disposes _forge3dActive (:31752) and is cross-disposed BY forge3d_open (:31676) — symmetric, one WebGL context ever. The mock ran THREE separate rAF loops (animBay, hlPulse, animSweep); the port COLLAPSES all of them into a single bayTick(now) called from the socket's ONE loop(), so nothing survives dispose. bayDispose() frees every CanvasTexture + the env CubeTexture (material.dispose() does NOT free a material's .map — tracked explicitly in _bayTex). §F MOBILE BUDGET: pixelRatio capped at 2; the ghost-reflection clone is gated behind BAY_REFLECT (auto-off above DPR 2.1, the pre-approved fallback — no re-ask per §F); motes 220 -> 120 above DPR 2. THIS SHIP: ghost reflection SHIPPED (BAY_REFLECT true at DPR<=2.1); note which path renders on John's device. NOTED, NOT FIXED, PRE-EXISTING (surfaced during the port, owed its own ship): the FLAT rack-elevation CSS is keyed on the EDP vocabulary (switch/pdu/storage/compute...) while master_rackToElevation emits master_hostType RAW codes (sw/pwr/stor/...); their intersection is ONLY 'gpu', so on a Master-loaded rack the FLAT elevation colours GPUs and leaves everything else default-styled. The Bay reads _TMAP->TYPE_COLOR and is therefore MORE correct than FLAT until that fix lands — so 3D and FLAT will DISAGREE on non-GPU colours, and that disagreement is the elevation's bug, not the Bay's. Recorded so it is understood, not mistaken for a .255 regression. Gates: node --check 4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS; CSS brace balance 3856/3856 IDENTICAL (no CSS this ship); CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0); UTF-8 box-draw round-trip verified; single-definition check on scene/camera/renderer/canvas/fitDist/rackGrp/trayMeshes/hlBand (each exactly 1 in reh3d); old .218 trayHex/FOV-32 gone; three-stamp lockstep .254 -> .255. ?legacy=1 BYTE-IDENTICAL: rackElevation_render3D is reachable only via the reh3d 3D toggle, which is emitted only under body.rd (see .254); the legacy house never enters this function. deploy_classifyDevice (persisted EDP/CSV path) untouched. BATCH: opens at 5 (.251/.252/.253/.254 + .255). ⚠️ .251-.254 device-verifies were cleared by John ('all good', then 'build it') BEFORE this ship; .255 is a NEW unverified ship and the batch cap is 6 — one slot remains before a hard device pass. DEVICE-VERIFY (John, iPhone, per the handoff): [A] FLAT default intact; toggle 3D -> bay loads, rack renders with LIVE Master trays (not the mock's GB300 loadout). [B] Rig matches the locked mock side-by-side (dark, edge-lit, env 0.25) — compare with mocks/MOCKUP-INSPECT3D-BAY-LOCKED.html. [C] Scan wave climbs ~6.5s, trays illuminate as it passes, halo wraps. [D] Scrub -> highlight band + edge box glow, breathing, unmissable. [E] Ghost fleet + glass platform + motes present; RACK READS AS THE STAR FROM EVERY ANGLE (if any bay element out-shines the rack, that element is wrong). [F] Tap a tray -> flat device detail opens; orbit-drag does NOT trigger it. [G] ⭐ TRAY COLOUR: an UNKNOWN-type tray reads MAGENTA, a MEDIA CONV tray reads SLATE, a PDU reads GOLD — the deviation call above; confirm you want type-coloured trays and NOT the mock's uniform piano-black. [H] An hgtUnknown tray (SPARKS gpu-b40-02 / cpu-gp2-* / fs-media-converter-chassis) shows the GOLD hazard hatch in 3D. [I] Toggle FLAT -> GL disposed clean; open FORGE after -> no dual-GL crash; and FORGE-first then 3D -> no crash. [J] Frame rate on-device acceptable; note if the reflection clone strained it (BAY_REFLECT can auto-drop). [K] Kill/relaunch PWA offline -> bay still renders. [L] ?legacy=1 -> toggle absent, unchanged. STILL OWED — JOHN'S RULING (unchanged): DFW02 empty-model row @c1:001:38 - heights for gpu-b40-02(160)/cpu-gp2-*(165)/inf-med-01(60)/om2216-c14(16)/fs-media-converter-chassis(5)/VAST DBox(50)/net-ufm-05 - whether the .252 magenta reads too loud - AND NEW: the FLAT-elevation EDP-vocabulary colour bug above (owed its own ship).
